title: Cancel, Interrupt, And Input

external_signals.action: Provider process SIGTERM -> drain -> SIGKILL fallback. Remaining drafts drain before close.

external_signals.signal: local daemon stop / supervisor context cancel
external_signals.action: RunController treats in-flight provider run as cancelled, requests process cancel, records TaskCancelled transition event and best-effort WorkdirArchived, then deregisters runtime.
Provider-side interrupt message, such as Claude interrupt or Codex turn/interrupt; process remains alive.
Send response through provider stdin / RPC; resulting provider output flows as drafts.
Send provider approval protocol response, such as Codex app-server approval/resolved.

consumer_requirements.reason: IPC shutdown requests parse the shared lifecycle vocabulary
supervisor stop uses shared default timeout policy
provider process kill receives lifecycle timeout and authority

assertions: shutdown authority vocabulary is owned by pkg/lifecycle
shutdown authority order is none < graceful < forced
default shutdown timeout policy is owned by pkg/lifecycle
daemon, supervisor, runtime actor, and session process kill consume lifecycle instead of redefining authority
